title: 1920s Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin really was unusually experimental in the 1920s:** cabaret, cinema, queer nightlife, art and political satire all flourished.
**The glamour was built on instability:** war defeat, revolution, hyperinflation and street politics shaped the same city as the clubs.
**Greater Berlin changed everything in 1920:** the city grew to around **3.8 million** people and became a true world metropolis.
**The classic Golden Twenties window was short:** the stable cultural boom mostly ran from about **1924 to 1929**.
**The Nazi takeover destroyed the scene:** in **1933**, cabarets closed, artists fled, and Hirschfeld's Institute for Sexual Science was looted and its books burned.
